name: Amanat Acquisition Corp.
business: Amanat Acquisition Corp. is a blank check company formed to effect a merger or business combination with one or more businesses, intending to focus on healthcare or healthcare-related industries, particularly biotechnology and life sciences.

exposures.sentence: While we may pursue a business combination target in any business or industry, we intend to focus on healthcare or healthcare-related industries, which can benefit from the expertise and capabilities of our management team in order to create long-term shareholder value.
Our primary focus will be on the biotechnology and life science sectors.

catalysts.sentence: Class A ordinary shares offered: 7,500,000 shares (or 8,625,000 shares if the underwriter's over-allotment option is exercised in full), at $10.00 per share.

catalysts.event: Deadline to consummate initial business combination
catalysts.sentence: We have until the date that is 24 months from the closing of this offering or until such earlier liquidation date as our board of directors may approve, to consummate our initial business combination.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. annual national health expenditures grew to $5.3 trillion in 2024 and are projected to reach approximately 20.3% of GDP by 2033.
tamClaims.sentence: The total U.S. annual national health expenditures grew to $5.3 trillion in 2024 according to the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, which also projects that total healthcare spending will reach approximately 20.3% of total U.S. gross domestic product (GDP) by 2033, as compared to 18.0% of GDP in 2024.

tamClaims.claim: There were 245 FDA approvals between 2020 and 2024, with 46 additional approvals in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: There has also been a gradual increase in U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approval of new drugs, from 158 approvals between 2010 and 2014 to 245 approvals between 2020 and 2024, with 46 additional approvals in 2025 reflecting a sustained elevated level of activity that compares favorably to the historic average of 36 approvals per year since 1993.

tamClaims.claim: Average cost to bring a therapy to market is $1.0 billion to $2.3 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: Bringing a single therapy to market now requires an average investment of approximately $1.0 billion to $2.3 billion, accounting for the costs of the many drug candidates that fail during development.

tamClaims.claim: Over 100 biotech companies had completed crossover financing rounds yet remained private as of Q4 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: As of the fourth quarter of 2025, there were over 100 biotech companies that had completed crossover financing rounds yet remained private, according to Pitchbook data.
